Function reference

Signal Processing

signal_bind()
Bind
signal_correct()
Baseline Correction
signal_drift()
Drift Intensities
signal_mean()
Mean Intensities
signal_shift()
Shift the X Scale
signal_select() signal_slice()
Subset

Baseline Estimation

baseline_linear()
Linear Baseline Estimation
baseline_peakfilling()
4S Peak Filling
baseline_polynomial()
Polynomial Baseline Estimation
baseline_rollingball()
Rolling Ball Baseline Estimation
baseline_rubberband()
Rubberband Baseline Estimation
baseline_snip()
SNIP Baseline Estimation

Smoothing

smooth_likelihood()
Penalized Likelihood Smoothing
smooth_loess()
Loess Smoothing
smooth_rectangular()
Rectangular Smoothing
smooth_savitzky()
Savitzky-Golay Filter
smooth_triangular()
Triangular Smoothing
smooth_whittaker()
Whittaker Smoothing

Peaks Detection

peaks_find()
Find Peaks
peaks_fwhm()
Half-Width at Half-Maximum

Integration

integrate_rectangle()
Rectangle Rule
integrate_trapezoid()
Trapezoidal Rule

Normalization

rescale_area()
Normalize intensities by AUC
rescale_range() rescale_min() rescale_max()
Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum
rescale_total()
Rescale intensities to sum to a specified value
rescale_transform()
Transform Intensities

Resample

resample_bin()
Bin
resample_down()
Downsample
resample_interpolate()
Linearly Interpolate

Windows

window_sliding()
Sliding Windows
window_tumbling()
Tumbling Windows

Replacement

replace_negative()
Replace Negative Values
replace_threshold replace_threshold-method replace_threshold,numeric,numeric,function-method replace_threshold,ANY,missing,function-method replace_threshold,numeric,numeric,numeric-method replace_threshold,ANY,missing,numeric-method
Replace Values Below a Given Threshold

Specialized Tools

ka2_strip_penalized()
Strip XRD ka2

Datasets

BEGe
Gamma-Ray Spectrometry
LaBr
Gamma-Ray Spectrometry
XRD
Powder X-ray Diffraction

On this page

alkahest is a part of the tesselle project, a collection of packages for research and teaching in archaeology. Learn more at tesselle.org.

Developed by Nicolas Frerebeau.

Site built with pkgdown 2.0.7.
